prompt: A vertical Shanshui landscape representing gradient descent as a sequence of mist-filled mountain basins. Executed in hand-ground carbon ink on aged silk. Use Sanyuan (Three Distances) composition, with towering high-distance peaks descending through layered middle ground into deep-distance valleys. Depict the optimization path as a Feibai (Flying White) brushstroke winding downward toward the lowest global minimum. Structure the rock faces with sharp axe-cut Cun-fa texture strokes. Use liu-bai voids and diluted ink washes to imply an abstract high-dimensional loss landscape. Strictly avoid linear perspective and photographic shading. Monochrome ink-wash palette only, with a small red cinnabar artist seal in the lower left. --ar 2:3 --v 6.1
tradition_approach: Applies Sanyuan depth, Cun-fa texture, Feibai brushwork, and liu-bai voids so the concept reads through authentic monochrome Shanshui spatial grammar.
